description:
GOAL: Calls actually allocate frames. Recursion works. Tail-self-recursion stays bounded.

## Decisions captured

- Per Term::Call: codegen emits fz_alloc(schema_id, size) -> *mut HeapHeader, fills in the frame's fields (continuation pointer, captured locals), returns the new frame_ptr.
- Per Term::TailCall: REUSE the caller's frame — overwrite locals + return new frame_ptr (essentially the same frame_ptr if shapes match; new alloc if larger schema).
- Generated Cranelift fns know their frame schema (from .11.6) and emit field-offset access at load/store sites.
- Frame schema is registered in .11.2 SchemaRegistry; codegen treats it as a static constant (offset table) at lowering time.
- Codegen MUST agree with the GC tracer on field offsets — same Schema is used by both. Mismatch = silent corruption.

## Deliverables

- Per-call lowering in src/ir_codegen.rs: alloc frame via runtime fn, write args + continuation, return ptr.
- Tail-call lowering: detect Term::TailCall, reuse the caller's frame (rewrite fields, return).
- Frame access helpers: load/store at known offsets for each local.
- Runtime fz_alloc fn (callable from JIT) that defers to .11.2 Heap::alloc.

## Tests

- `fn add1(n), do: n + 1; main: print(add1(41))` -> 42.
- `fn fact(0): 1; fn fact(n): n * fact(n-1); main: print(fact(10))` -> 3628800.
- `fn count(0,acc): acc; fn count(n,acc): count(n-1, acc+1); main: print(count(100000, 0))` -> 100000. CRITICAL TEST: must not allocate per-call (tail-call frame reuse) and must not stack-overflow.
- Mutual recursion: `even / odd` for small N.

## Risks

- Frame schema mismatch between codegen and tracer = silent corruption. Mitigation: single source of truth in SchemaRegistry; FieldDescriptor offsets computed once.
- Tail-call frame reuse correctness: if callee schema is LARGER than caller, must alloc new frame; if smaller-or-equal, can reuse. Static decision.

## Out of scope

- Tier-up integration / interp marshaling (.11.9).
- Heap types beyond scalars (.11.10+).
- Frame inlining (.11.15).
title: Cranelift codegen — frame allocation via schemas
